study: Sangram SG-SE-005 (Локатив) — locative + the locative absolute (native candidate + adjudicated floor)
kind: beyond-quota core ① (opening set already 19/19); native positive with adversarially-checked honesty framing

snapshot.provenance_note: pin 04e0778 orphaned; binding = provenance table + SHA-256 + tag c3-pin-04e0778-content

locative_absolute_candidate.candidate_note: 20,973 is the CANDIDATE / morphological upper bound (every Loc participle); it also contains attributive and substantival Loc participles — the absolute is NOT natively flagged

adjudication_on_parsed_subset.note: of the ~557 Loc participles carrying a deprel, advcl:temp/advcl/advcl:cond dominate (adverbial clause = the absolute); acl (attributive) + obl (argument) are the non-absolute residue. Extrapolating the ~65%/~17% split, roughly 14-17% of the 20,973 candidates are likely non-absolute. High-precision FLOOR = 367 advcl*-tagged + 246 subject-participle pairs.

traditional_layer.witness: Whitney 1889 §§303-305 (locative uses), §§303b (locative absolute / sati saptamī); Pāṇini 2.3.36-37 (adhikaraṇa) + 2.3.37 (bhāve / sati saptamī)

limits.candidate_not_construction: loc participle (morphology) != locative absolute (construction); 20,973 is an upper bound
limits.deprel_partial: the dependency layer covers only ~2.76%% of the candidates (and 74/270 texts); the absolute/attributive/argument split is native only on that subset
limits.tense_sparse: feat_tense is None on 16,467/20,973 participles, so past-vs-present absolute cannot be totalled from feat_tense
limits.time_vs_place_not_native: the locative's time-vs-place split is NOT a native field (m_wordsem is a numeric sense id); any such figure is an inferred lemma heuristic
limits.pin: orphaned 04e0778, bound by provenance table + SHA-256 + tag
